_doc: Data-driven league registry. A league INHERITS its config from its `extends` chain (league -> intermediate profile -> family). Nearest definition wins; objects shallow-merge, scalars replace. The worker resolves the chain at load and uses it to interpret raw ESPN period integers, pick the right normalizer, and humanize labels. Adding a league = a data entry, not code. All `espnLeagueId` values are STRINGS sourced from live JSON; `verifiedIds:false` marks ids/structures the adversarial pass could NOT confirm against the live API (treat as best-effort, re-fetch before trusting).
_capabilitiesDoc: capabilities{} = the render-or-hide gate (SCHEMA.md §2a; build-spec Part I §5): per-family boolean flags naming which OPTIONAL data tier/field a sport actually serves, resolved through the same extends chain as everything else (objects shallow-merge, so a league override ADDS flags without restating the family's). OMIT-MEANS-FALSE: consumers read `capabilities.flag == true` and hide the element cleanly otherwise — never an empty chip. Sourced from schema/espn-guide/ observed presence (crawl 2026-07-08), not from documentation. hasLineScores and rankingsFeed predate this object and stay top-level keys — do NOT duplicate them here.

client._doc: Advisory app-update gate, echoed onto /v1/health (internal _-prefixed keys are stripped on the wire; see worker/src/client.js). The app compares its baked versionCode (the GitHub Actions run_number that also stamps the APK's versionCode — see .github/workflows/release.yml + app/lib/src/version.dart) against these numbers: minVersionCode<=code<recommendedVersionCode => a dismissible 'update available' banner; code<minVersionCode => a persistent 'no longer supported' banner; otherwise nothing. Comparison is by versionCode (monotonic), NOT the semver name. ABSENT block (old worker / fork / offline mock) MUST mean allow (fail-open). 0/0 keeps the gate fully inert. RELEASE: the release workflow auto-injects recommendedVersionCode (=run_number) and latestVersionName (=tag) at deploy time, AFTER the APK Release publishes — so these committed values are just the baseline a manual `wrangler deploy` would use. minVersionCode is never auto-bumped; raise it in a commit to retire an old build. See schema/SCHEMA.md §11.

standings: https://site.api.espn.com/apis/v2/sports/{espnPath}/standings?season={year}  (USE apis/v2, NOT apis/site/v2 — the latter returns a fullViewLink stub)

australian-football.score: competitor.score is TOTAL points as a string (goals*6 + behinds), e.g. '120'. The goals.behinds breakdown (e.g. '19.6.120') is NOT a discrete competitor field on the cheap scoreboard; it is derivable only from per-player leaders.goals — surface total points as the canonical score.
australian-football.linescores: competitor.linescores present and verified = per-quarter points, NON-cumulative (sum the quarters to get the total; do NOT treat as cumulative like rugby).
australian-football.clock: counts UP; ~20-min quarters plus time-on, so displayClock can exceed 20:00 (verified 29:27 in Q4). format.regulation.periods=4.
australian-football.standings: AFL ladder is ordered by Premiership Points (points: 4/win, 2/draw, 0/loss) THEN Percentage (pointsFor/pointsAgainst*100) — NOT point differential. pointDifferential exists in the feed but is not the tiebreaker.
australian-football.overtimeDetect: period>4 (rare; AFL home-and-away & most finals are drawn-allowed, but some finals go to extra time — extra quarters append linescore elements). Detect via status.period > regulationPeriods.
edgeCases: draw(home-and-away games can finish level — ties are real, hence W-L-D ladder)
notes: timed team sport, mirrors basketball/football discriminators
score is total points; goals.behinds split not on cheap scoreboard

lacrosse.overtimeDetect: period>4 (sudden-death OT appends one linescore; PLL final showed period 5, detail Final/OT). format.regulation.periods=4 VERIFIED.
lacrosse.lineScoresPerPeriod: QUIRK vs rugby: linescores[].value is PER-PERIOD (NOT cumulative) — periods SUM to competitor.score (verified NCAAM 3+8+2+3=16, PLL 1+2+0+1=4).
lacrosse.statusFinalDetail: Final detail is Final (NOT FT); OT games read Final/OT.
notes: 4 leagues report 4 quarters on the cheap scoreboard with per-quarter linescores + score + record (W-L), live-verified
NCAA women traditionally play two 30-min halves but ESPN reports 4 quarter linescores — model follows the live data
period length differs by league (NLL/PLL ~12-15min) but is not surfaced by the feed

volleyball.setBased: VERIFIED: best-of-5, win 3. competitor.score = SETS WON (numeric, e.g. '3'); periodScores[]/linescores[] = per-set POINTS (race-to-25 for sets 1-4, race-to-15 for the deciding set 5), each set carrying its own winner bool. Match has NO clock and NO overtime — the 5th set IS the decider. 3-5 periods played; period count = sets contested, not fixed at 5.
notes: set-based like tennis but competitorKind=team and homeAway is meaningful
NEVER sum per-set points for the match score — match score is sets won, read from competitor.score
clockDirection none: sets are race-to-points, not timed
college carries curatedRank (AVCA poll); set ranking=curatedRank per-league

water-polo.score: competitor.score is a numeric string ('10'); per-quarter competitor.linescores[] present (value per period) on covered events — sampled men's final had linescores:null (sparse college coverage), women's events DO carry them.
water-polo.overtimeDetect: period>4 (overtime is timed extra play). Family default regulationPeriods=4.
notes: Only NCAA men's(1925)/women's(1926) leagues exist on ESPN site API; FINA m/w slugs 400.

4 quarters VERIFIED via format.regulation.periods=4 on both leagues. periodLengthMin=8 is the NCAA standard (8-min quarters) but NOT confirmed from API (no clock in format.regulation) — treat as best-effort.
standingsColumns are a best-effort default — live standings endpoint returned only children/seasons with no flat entries (W-L unverified).

field-hockey.overtime: NOT a structured field on the thin scoreboard payload. OT (golden-goal extra time) and shootout (SO) are conveyed ONLY via status.type.detail/shortDetail/altDetail ('Final/OT','Final/2OT','Final/SO') — branch on status.type.name + detail string, never on a period count.
field-hockey.overtimeDetect: status.type.detail/altDetail contains 'OT'/'SO' (e.g. 'Final/2OT'). No linescores to count, no period>4 signal on the cheap payload.
_notes: VERIFIED 2026-06-15 against field-hockey/womens-college-field-hockey (id 802): format.regulation.periods=4; competitors have NO linescores array; competitor.score is a plain numeric goal string ('1'); status.type.detail='Final/2OT' on an OT game.

Modern FIH field hockey is 4x15-min quarters (since 2019); ESPN reports periods=4. clockDirection 'up' is inferred (match clock counts up like soccer/rugby) — UNVERIFIED, no live in-progress game observed.
hasLineScores=false: render score only (no per-quarter grid), matching the cheap thin payload. SO/OT shown as a status badge from status.type.detail.
standingsColumns are best-effort goal-table defaults (W-D-L/GD/PTS) — NOT verified against a live field-hockey standings endpoint.

notes: clock counts up, stoppage shown as 45'+x / 90'+x
no per-half linescores array; derive halves from details[]

football.overtimeDetect: period>4 OR linescores.length>4 OR altDetail contains 'OT'. QUIRK: STATUS_FINAL_OVERTIME does NOT exist — OT games are STATUS_FINAL(id3) with detail 'Final/OT' and altDetail 'OT'. Detail suffix is unreliable (7OT showed plain 'Final').

basketball.overtime: uniform 5-min (format.overtime.clock=300s), unlimited until a winner; each OT appends one linescores element. Detect via period>regulation.
basketball.overtimeDetect: status.period > regulationPeriods OR shortDetail contains 'OT' (altDetail '2OT' also present)
notes: period LENGTH is the key per-league delta; use format.regulation.clock (NBA 720s, WNBA/NCAAW 600s, NCAAM 1200s/half)

baseball.extraScoreFields: competitor.hits and competitor.errors complete the R/H/E line; period = inning number
baseball.liveLabel: inning half is in status.detail ('Top 5th'/'Bottom 7th'/'Mid 3rd'/'End 6th') + situation.isTopInning, NOT in status.period
notes: a 'Final' with fewer than regulation innings is a legitimate called/mercy game, not an error

hockey.shootout: NHL reg season ONLY: period 5, detail 'Final/SO', altDetail 'SO'; the SO decider (+1) is folded into competitor.score. College has NO shootout period.
hockey.overtimeDetect: period>3. QUIRK: period 5 = SHOOTOUT for NHL reg season, but = 2OT for NHL playoffs / NCAA tournament — disambiguate by league + seasonType.
notes: NHL records 'W-L-OTL'; college records 'W-L-T' (ties real)
NHL summary has NO header key — read status from scoreboard, not summary

tennis.bestOf: DO NOT trust format.regulation.periods (VERIFIED unreliable: returns 5 for non-major qualifying & doubles). Derive: men's singles at a Slam (events.major && mens-singles) = best-of-5; everything else = best-of-3. Validate against actual linescores length.
tennis.tiebreak: periodScores[].tiebreak is present on BOTH competitors = each side's TB points. Reconstruct '7-6(3)' from the loser's value. Can exceed 10 (11-9 super-TBs); never key off ==10.
tennis.standings: none — tour RANKINGS list, not a table; within a tournament advancement is a single-elim bracket
tennis.rankingsNote: VERIFIED 2026-07: site apis/site/v2 .../rankings works for atp(150 ranks, points+trend, inline athletes) and wta — same endpoint as college polls, athlete-shaped ranks. /summary is DEAD for tennis (400 for every event/competition id permutation incl. web + core hosts) — match stats have NO source; do not re-add a tennis summary branch without a new probe.

golf.progress: competitor.status.thru (holes done) + status.period (round). Score is to-par string ('-10'/'E'/'+3'); strokes total is derived by summing completed-round linescore values (normalize.js).
golf.playoffDetect: competition.status.hadPlayoff === true (the reliable signal) AND/OR competitor.status.playoff AND/OR a linescore period>numberOfRounds. QUIRK: SITE tournament.playoffType is HALLUCINATED; the CORE event carries playoffType {id:-1,'None'} but hadPlayoff stays the signal.
golf.nonFinishers: VERIFIED 2026-07: NO per-player MC/WD/DQ label survives on any live endpoint — competitor.status is absent on the site scoreboard (cut players are simply REMOVED from later-round payloads: 144 competitors during R1-2, 72 after the cut) and the leaderboard endpoints 404. Render the cut LINE from meta.golf instead; do not hunt for status labels.
golf.meta: meta.golf {numberOfRounds,currentRound,cutRound,cutScore,cutCount,major,scoringSystem} — VERIFIED 2026-07 from CORE event -> tournament.$ref -> tournaments/{id}/seasons/{yyyy}. NOT on the site scoreboard. The worker's scores route enriches golf events with 2 extra fetches each (best-effort; omitted on failure). Rewrite $ref '.pvt' hosts to '.com'.
golf.scorecard: hole-by-hole per player: site.web.api.espn.com/apis/site/v2/sports/golf/{tour}/leaderboard/{eventId}/playersummary?season={yyyy}&player={id} (VERIFIED 2026-07: per-hole value+par+scoreType.name, inScore/outScore, teeTime/startTee/groupNumber pre-round, profile+stats). Served as /v1/scorecard/{sport}/{league}/{eventId}/{playerId}. Golf seasons are calendar-aligned so a getFullYear() season fallback is safe HERE (unlike standings).
golf.scoringSystem: 'Medal' (stroke play) vs 'Teamstroke' (two-player team events, e.g. Dow Championship)
notes: cut presence varies BY EVENT not league — read cutRound off the core tournament resource (0 = no cut)
ignore trailing sentinel linescore periods (e.g. 402) with only {period}
scoreboard per-hole linescores exist but carry only value+scoreType.displayValue (no par, no named scoreType) — the playersummary is the scorecard source

racing.result: no score field. Finish = competitor.order; grid = competitor.startOrder; period = laps completed by leader. championship pts + lap stats in core statistics.
racing.dnf: DNF/unclassified = competitor has NO `order` (and a SPARSE statistics set — keys like lapsCompleted/place are ABSENT, not zero). Never test lapsCompleted==0; key off absence of order. Vehicle object still present.
racing.multiCompetition: F1 event = MANY competitions (FP1/FP2/FP3 share type.id=1, differ by abbreviation; Qualifying; Race; Sprint weekends add 2 more). NASCAR/IndyCar = ONE competition (id==eventId). Filter F1 to type.abbreviation=='Race' for the race.
racing.liveFlag: competition.status.flag (GREEN/YELLOW/RED/CHECKER) is F1-ONLY; absent for NASCAR/IndyCar.
racing.standings: VERIFIED 2026-07: the SAME site apis/v2 standings path every team sport uses works for racing — F1 children[]=Driver Standings(athlete-shaped entries)+Constructor Standings(team-shaped); NASCAR one flat 'Standings' child (athlete-shaped). Entries carry rank+championshipPts+per-race columns; the core rankings endpoint is EMPTY (count 0) — don't chase it.

mma.card: event = a fight CARD; each competition = one bout (red/blue corner via order). Per-bout format.regulation.periods is 3 (regular) or 5 (title/main) — READ PER BOUT.
mma.method: Outcome = winner boolean + method. QUIRK: method (KO/TKO, Submission, Decision) is NOT in the site scoreboard status — only via core API status $ref / details[].type.text. Decision finishTime is the round length ('5:00'), not a stoppage.
mma.siteVsCore: site scoreboard may DROP a scratched bout (UFC 324: site 10 vs core 11 competitions) — site count is authoritative for display; never align by core matchNumber.
mma.format: site scoreboard format.regulation = {periods} ONLY; round length (clock) is core API only.
mma.boxingUnsupported: ESPN has NO boxing sport. Only sparse 'boxe'/'k1' league slugs under mma. Source boxing elsewhere.

cricket.format: READ PER MATCH from competitions[].class.generalClassCard — present broadly (NOT internationals-only): 'Twenty20'(IPL,20ov/2inn), 'Women T20', 'ODI'(50ov/2inn), 'T20I', 'First-class'/'Test'(up to 4 innings, multi-day). class.eventType is the coarse bucket (T20/ODI/Test). A single tour id can contain matches of DIFFERENT formats.
cricket.score: competitor.score is a COMPOSITE STRING ('161/5 (18/20 ov, target 156)'); authoritative numbers in periodScores[].cricket {runs,wickets,overs,isBatting,target,reason}. reason ∈ {'all out','target reached','complete','No result'}.
cricket.result: status.summary carries the result ('RCB won by 5 wkts (12b rem)' / 'AUS Women won by 65 runs' / 'Match drawn' / 'No result'). Winner via summary, not run totals.
edgeCases: suspended(multi-day 'Stumps Day N', state stays 'in' across days)
notes: bilateral tours get a NEW ephemeral numeric league id per series
URL slug != leagues[0].id — read the real id from JSON

rugby.lineScoresCumulative: QUIRK: rugby linescores[].value is a CUMULATIVE running total — period 2 ALWAYS == final score; period 1 == score at half time (may be 0 if not backfilled). NEVER sum period1+period2. ESPN also emits placeholder periods 20 & 60 (always 0) — ignored via ignorePeriods.
rugby.overtime: knockouts: 2x10 extra time then sudden-death + kicking competition; pool/league draws allowed
rugby.standings: stats gamesWon/Lost/Drawn, points, pointsFor/Against, bonusPoints, pointsDifference

rugby-league.overtime: golden point: 2x5 extra time, first score wins (reg season may still draw)
rugby-league.statusFinalDetail: QUIRK: rugby-league Final detail is 'Final' (NOT union's 'FT') — terminal detection keying on 'FT' misses every NRL game
notes: separate ESPN sport path 'rugby-league'; requires a league id segment (e.g. /3/) — bare path 404s

soccer.domestic.overtime: none — a draw is a final result (STATUS_FULL_TIME, 'FT')

soccer.knockout.overtime: 2x15 extra time (period 4, AET, STATUS_FINAL_AET id45) then penalty shootout (period 5, FT-Pens, STATUS_FINAL_PEN id47)
soccer.knockout.decisionFields: shootoutScore (number, winner set from it); aggregateScore (STRING) + advance(bool) for TWO-LEG ties (UEFA only)
soccer.knockout.standings: groups (children[] per group) and/or knockout bracket

soccer/usa.1.name: Major League Soccer
soccer/usa.1._note: reg season draws stand; playoff shootout format UNVERIFIED against live data

soccer/uefa.champions.name: UEFA Champions League
soccer/uefa.champions._note: new 36-team league phase + knockout; two-leg ties carry aggregateScore(STRING)+advance

soccer/fifa.world.name: FIFA World Cup
soccer/fifa.world._note: 2026 standings returned 8 groups A-H (enumerate children[] at runtime; do not hardcode group count); group stage draws stand, knockout adds AET+pens

soccer/_other.name: Other domestic top divisions (ned.1, sco.1, por.1, etc.)
soccer/_other._note: all share soccer.domestic; read id from JSON. Cups (eng.fa, copa_del_rey) extend soccer.knockout

football/nfl.name: NFL
football/nfl.overtime: one 10-min(reg)/15-min(playoff) modified sudden-death (period 5); reg season can TIE

football/college-football.name: NCAA Football (FBS)
football/college-football.overtime: untimed alternating-possession from 25; 2pt-only from 3OT; never ties; period 5,6,7...
football/college-football._note: leagues[].groups is an OBJECT {id,name,shortName,isConference} when ?groups= passed; /rankings endpoint for AP/Coaches/CFP

football/ufl.name: United Football League
football/ufl.overtime: alternating single 2pt conversion, best-of-3 rounds then sudden death

football/cfl.name: Canadian Football League
football/cfl.overtime: alternating possession from 35; reg season caps at 2 → can tie
football/cfl._note: 3-DOWN football, 110-yd field: situation.down maxes at 3, same field names. WARNING: ?dates= unreliable for CFL (returns stale 2023 calendar); live capture needs core.api path

basketball/mens-college-basketball.name: NCAA Men's Basketball
basketball/mens-college-basketball._note: capabilities.hasSeeds merges ONTO the basketball-family flags (core tournamentMatchup.seed, NCAA tournament only). THE delta: only basketball league in HALVES (verified format.regulation.periods=2, clock=1200s); OT periods 3+; player box score lacks +/- (13 cols vs NBA 14)

basketball/womens-college-basketball.name: NCAA Women's Basketball
basketball/womens-college-basketball._note: capabilities.hasSeeds merges onto the family flags (NCAA tournament seeds). VERIFIED quarters since 2015-16 (matches WNBA, NOT NCAAM) — the most error-prone assumption, confirmed against 2025 title game

basketball/nbl.name: National Basketball League
basketball/nbl._note: Australian/NZ NBL (uid s:40~l:55; includes NZ Breakers). THE delta vs basketball family default 12-min: 10-min quarters (format.regulation.clock=600s) — same as WNBA/NCAAW. Quarters VERIFIED (format.regulation.slug='quarter', 4 linescores per final). 5-min OT clock VERIFIED (format.overtime.clock=300s); unlimited/one-linescore-per-OT inherited from family, NOT directly observed (no OT game in sampled finals). No curatedRank (ranking=none). UNPROBED: playoff/Grand Final shapes (season.type=3, possible best-of series) and standings ladder — inherit family defaults until a finals game is captured.

baseball/mlb.name: Major League Baseball
baseball/mlb._note: id is 10 NOT 1 (1 is the sport segment s:1); Manfred runner on 2B in extras not a field; doubleheaders = 2 events same date

baseball/college-baseball.name: NCAA Baseball
baseball/college-baseball._note: scheduled doubleheaders may be 7 innings — a 'complete' game can legitimately have 7 linescores; season.type can be 6 (championship-series, CWS)

baseball/college-softball.name: NCAA Softball
baseball/college-softball._note: CRITICAL path is baseball/college-softball (no top-level softball sport). 7-inning regulation (format.regulation.periods=7) is the key delta; International Tiebreaker runner on 2B; 8-runs-after-5 mercy

hockey/nhl.name: NHL
hockey/nhl.standings: Eastern/Western conference children[]; order by points, RW, ROW, H2H, GD
hockey/nhl._note: reg season: 5-min 3-on-3 OT(period4) then shootout(period5,'Final/SO'); playoffs: unlimited 20-min OT, no SO. plays type ids NOT verified — do not assert 506-509

hockey/mens-college-hockey.name: NCAA Men's Ice Hockey
hockey/mens-college-hockey._note: ties possible; boxscore/plays often EMPTY for college; tournament uses 20-min OT, no shootout period

hockey/womens-college-hockey.name: NCAA Women's Hockey
hockey/womens-college-hockey._note: ties common (e.g. 35-1-0); sparse coverage — many in-season dates return events:[]

tennis/atp.name: ATP Tour
tennis/atp._note: id is 851 NOT 850 (850 = tennis SPORT id, shared s:850). Men's singles best-of-5 at Slams only

tennis/wta.name: WTA Tour
tennis/wta._note: all singles best-of-3 incl Slams. Combined events appear under BOTH atp & wta endpoints

golf/pga.name: PGA Tour
golf/pga._note: 72-hole/4 rounds, cut after R2 (cutRound=2); signature/no-cut events cutRound=0

golf/champions-tour.name: PGA Tour Champions
golf/champions-tour._note: 54-hole/3 rounds (numberOfRounds=3) and NO cut (cutRound=0) — the key golf delta

racing/f1.name: Formula 1
racing/f1._note: MULTI-competition event (FP1/FP2/FP3/Qual/Race; sprint weekends +2). Only league with working per-competitor status endpoint + competition.status.flag; /teams returns the constructors (hasTeams)

racing/nascar-premier.name: NASCAR Cup Series
racing/nascar-premier._note: slug MUST be nascar-premier (racing/nascar 400s). Single competition (id==eventId). Richest vehicle + stats. GWC overtime = more laps, not an OT structure

racing/irl.name: IndyCar
racing/irl._note: single competition; DNF = no order + sparse stats

mma/ufc.name: UFC
mma/ufc._note: richest combat data. VERIFIED 2026-07: site /summary 404s for EVERY MMA event (it proxies a broken core call) — the worker builds the rich tier from core per-bout status (structured result) + competitor linescores (judge scorecards) instead. Site /rankings works: 24 divisions + P4P, inline athletes with recordSummary/hasAccolade. Minor-promotion scoreboards (ksw/cage-warriors/lfa/pfl) all verified working — third-party docs claiming 400s are stale

cricket/8052.name: County Championship Div 1
cricket/8052._note: First-class (generalClassCard='First-class'), up to 4 innings, draws common

cricket/_tours.name: International bilateral tours (ODI/T20I/Test)
cricket/_tours._note: ephemeral per-series ids; READ format PER MATCH from class.generalClassCard, never per-league

rugby-league/3.name: NRL (National Rugby League)
rugby-league/3._note: path rugby-league/3/scoreboard works (bare path 404s); Final detail is 'Final' not 'FT'

soccer/ned.2.name: Dutch Keuken Kampioen Divisie
soccer/ned.2._note: second tier; promotion playoffs exist but base mode is annual table

soccer/sco.1.name: Scottish Premiership
soccer/sco.1._note: split-table after 33 rds but ESPN models as flat W-D-L table; soccer.domestic fine

soccer/bra.1.name: Brazilian Serie A
soccer/bra.1._note: round-robin annual table (seasonType 'Brasileiro Serie A'), NOT split-season — verified live

soccer/arg.1.name: Argentine Liga Profesional de Futbol
soccer/arg.1._note: split-season w/ finals — verified live seasonType 'Apertura - Final' (id5); knockout playoff phase

soccer/ksa.1.name: Saudi Pro League
soccer/ksa.1._note: straight annual table — verified live seasonType '2025-26 Saudi Pro League'

soccer/mex.1.name: Mexican Liga BBVA MX
soccer/mex.1._note: split-season (Apertura/Clausura) + liguilla — verified live seasonType 'Torneo Apertura'; knockout playoffs

soccer/mex.2.name: Mexican Liga de Expansion MX
soccer/mex.2._note: split-season w/ finals (seasonType 'Campeon de Campeones' observed)

soccer/bel.1.name: Belgian Pro League
soccer/bel.1._note: has championship/europe playoff rounds but ESPN models regular phase as table

soccer/jpn.1.name: Japanese J.League
soccer/jpn.1._note: primarily annual table; minor placement playoffs (seasonType 'Placement Playoffs' observed) inherit knockout OT via period detection if encountered

soccer/ind.1.name: Indian Super League
soccer/ind.1._note: league phase + playoffs/final — verified live seasonType 'Final' (id4)

soccer/aus.1.name: Australian A-League Men
soccer/aus.1._note: regular table + finals series — verified live seasonType 'Grand Final' (id4); knockout for finals

soccer/aus.w.1.name: Australian A-League Women
soccer/aus.w.1._note: regular table + finals series (mirrors A-League Men)

soccer/eng.2.name: English Championship
soccer/eng.2._note: annual table; promotion playoffs are a separate add-on, base mode is the table

soccer/par.1.name: Paraguayan Primera Division
soccer/par.1._note: Apertura/Clausura titles but ESPN models as table; promote to knockout if a finals phase is observed

soccer/fra.w.1.name: French Premiere Ligue
soccer/fra.w.1._note: women's top flight (Premiere Ligue, ex-D1 Feminine)

soccer/usa.nwsl.name: NWSL
soccer/usa.nwsl._note: regular season table + single-elim playoffs -> knockout (verified seasonType 'Regular Season' id1; playoffs add knockout phase)

soccer/can.w.nsl.name: Northern Super League
soccer/can.w.nsl._note: new Canadian women's league; table + playoffs

soccer/uefa.europa.conf.name: UEFA Conference League
soccer/uefa.europa.conf._note: new league phase + knockout; two-leg ties carry aggregateScore(STRING)+advance like UCL/UEL

soccer/uefa.euro.name: UEFA European Championship
soccer/uefa.euro._note: groups + knockout; group draws stand, knockout adds AET+pens

soccer/uefa.euro_u21.name: UEFA European Under-21 Championship
soccer/uefa.euro_u21._note: U21 final tournament (not the ephemeral qualifying slug); groups+knockout

soccer/conmebol.libertadores.name: CONMEBOL Libertadores
soccer/conmebol.libertadores._note: groups + two-leg knockout; aggregateScore(STRING)+advance on ties

soccer/concacaf.leagues.cup.name: Leagues Cup
soccer/concacaf.leagues.cup._note: MLS vs Liga MX; group + single-elim, ties go straight to pens (no ET) — period detection handles it

soccer/campeones.cup.name: Campeones Cup
soccer/campeones.cup._note: single match MLS Cup champ vs Liga MX champ

soccer/fifa.cwc.name: FIFA Club World Cup
soccer/fifa.cwc._note: 2025+ expanded 32-team groups + knockout; group draws stand, knockout AET+pens

soccer/fifa.shebelieves.name: SheBelieves Cup
soccer/fifa.shebelieves._note: invitational women's round-robin mini-tournament; modeled under knockout (single-table mini-event)

soccer/global.finalissima.name: CONMEBOL-UEFA Cup of Champions (Finalissima)
soccer/global.finalissima._note: single match Copa America champ vs EURO champ

soccer/afc.cup.name: AFC Champions League Two
soccer/afc.cup._note: slug is afc.cup but now branded 'AFC Champions League Two' per live name

soccer/eng.league_cup.name: English Carabao Cup
soccer/eng.league_cup._note: League Cup; semis two-leg, ties may go straight to pens

soccer/eng.trophy.name: English EFL Trophy
soccer/eng.trophy._note: group stage + knockout; ties go straight to pens

soccer/eng.charity.name: English FA Community Shield

hockey/hockey-world-cup.name: World Cup of Hockey
hockey/hockey-world-cup._note: International invitational tournament (group stage + knockout final, best-of-? in final). Live-confirmed id 3455: STATUS_FINAL game, team competitors, numeric scores, per-period linescores present. Last edition 2016; no scheduled return so dates return events:[] most of the year. Tournament OT = 20-min sudden-death (no reg-season shootout period); standings is bracket/group, NOT an annual W-D-L table.

hockey/olympics-mens-ice-hockey.name: Olympic Men's Ice Hockey
hockey/olympics-mens-ice-hockey._note: Olympic tournament (group stage round-robin + single-elim medal bracket). Live-confirmed id 20146: STATUS_FINAL Gold Medal Game (Milano Cortina 2026), team competitors, numeric scores, 4 period linescores (3 regulation + OT). competitionType ids 36=GM(medal game). Quadrennial — events:[] outside the ~2-week Olympic window. Tournament OT = sudden-death (no reg-season shootout); standings is group/bracket, NOT an annual table.

hockey/olympics-womens-ice-hockey.name: Olympic Women's Ice Hockey
hockey/olympics-womens-ice-hockey._note: Olympic tournament (group stage round-robin + single-elim medal bracket). Live-confirmed id 20147: 2 events STATUS_FINAL incl. Bronze Medal Game (Milano Cortina 2026), team competitors, numeric scores, 4 period linescores. competitionType ids 37=BM(bronze medal). Quadrennial — events:[] outside the Olympic window. Tournament OT = sudden-death (no reg-season shootout); standings is group/bracket, NOT an annual table.

golf/tgl.name: TGL

Overrides the golf-family discriminators inline. OTLosses column => OT possible.

field-hockey/womens-college-field-hockey.name: NCAA Women's Field Hockey
field-hockey/womens-college-field-hockey._note: Live-probed 2026-06-15: leagues[0].id=802, abbr CFHOC, slug womens-college-field-hockey (the ONLY league in core API field-hockey/leagues, count=1). format.regulation.periods=4 (4x ~15-min quarters per FIH rules). NO linescores array on competitors (competitor.linescores absent) -> hasLineScores false, no per-quarter grid. score is a plain numeric goal string ('1'). OT/shootout NOT a structured field -> read off status.type.detail ('Final/2OT' / 'Final/SO' / 'Final/OT'), exactly like the thin-payload pattern. competitor carries curatedRank (NCAA poll). Standings columns UNVERIFIED (scoreboard thin; no standings probe) -> family defaults are best-effort W-D-L/GD/PTS.
